data.flavor: The gun "that won the West", the .45 was the most common US sidearm throughout the 1800's. A single action weapon, it must be cocked before firing, although later models had a flattened hammer allowing the gun to be fired by "fanning" the hammer.
data.name: Common

data.flavor: Now the standard officer's sidearm for the U.S. Army, the M-2OOO served well in the Central American Wars.
data.name: Common

data.flavor: Rugged, reliable, with excellent stopping power. Another fine E.C.C. product from the United Germanies.
data.name: Common

data.flavor: The ubiquitous "Polymer-one-shot" cheap plastic pistol. Available in designer colors.
data.name: Common

data.flavor: Another Dai Lung cheapie, built for the Street.
data.name: Common

data.flavor: Designed as an alternate to the 1998 U.S. Army sidearm trials. A solid contender.
data.name: Common

data.flavor: A well-made autopistol with good range and accuracy. A professional's gun.
data.name: Common

data.flavor: The most commonplace police weapon for many years, the Colt .38 has many variants, including the smaller "Chief's Special". With their high reliability, there are many of these guns still in circulation.
data.name: Common

data.flavor: Designed as a "holdout" for law enforcement agents, the COP uses a unique revolving firing pin arrangement. It's small size makes it easily hidden.
data.name: Common

data.flavor: A light duty autopistol used as a holdout and "lady's gun".
data.name: Common

data.flavor: An excellent .44 revolver, used in home defense and police work. It's long barrel makes it hard to conceal.
data.name: Common

data.flavor: Designed for US Border Patrol use, the Combat Magnum is a popular choice among police officers. Its "small frame" and reliable action make it a best seller.
data.name: Common

data.flavor: A sturdy Solo's gun, used as a standard military sidearm in the U.S. and E.C.C.
data.name: Common

data.flavor: It's cheap. It's powerful. It blows up sometimes. What else do you want?
data.name: Common

data.flavor: Cheap Hong Kong knockoff, often used by boosters and other street trash.
data.name: Common

data.flavor: Standard Soviet military rifle, exported worldwide, particularly to Soviet client-states. Reliable, rugged, but rather difficult to control, the AK-47 is probably the most well known weapon of it's type in the world.
data.name: Common

data.flavor: Standard US military rifle since the 1960's, the M-16 has high accuracy and a staggering ROF. A built in "tumble" effect compensates for the light 5.56 round. Earlier M-16 models were cantankerous and unrealiable in the extreme, with an accuracy of 1, not 2. The AR-15 and the AR-180 are civilian models used by police and home defense.
data.name: Common

data.flavor: A plastic and carbon fiber update of the AKM, distributed throughout the remains of the Soviet Bloc.
data.name: Common

data.flavor: A bull pup configured rifle using high tech plastics and aluminum, the AUG is the wave of the future. The scope is built in, giving it great accuracy, while it's rugged plastic construction gives it reliability and strength.
data.name: Common

data.flavor: Standard US military rifle since the 1960's, the M-16 has high accuracy and a staggering ROF. A built in "tumble" effect compensates for the light 5.56 round. Earlier M-16 models were cantankerous and unrealiable in the extreme, with an accuracy of 1, not 2. The AR-15 and the AR-180 are civilian models used by police and home defense.
data.name: Common

data.flavor: Standard Soviet military rifle, exported worldwide, particularly to Soviet client-states. Reliable, rugged, but rather difficult to control, the AK-47 is probably the most well known weapon of it's type in the world.
data.name: Common

data.flavor: A basic scoped hunting rifle, used to hunt deer.
data.name: Common

data.flavor: A light, all purpose update, similar to the M-16B.
data.name: Common

data.flavor: Standard NATO rifle. A very deadly assault weapon; durable and handles well.
data.name: Common

data.flavor: Another Soviet retread, with improved sighting and lightened with composites.
data.name: Common

data.flavor: Standard Soviet military rifle, exported worldwide, particularly to Soviet client-states. Reliable, rugged, but rather difficult to control, the AK-47 is probably the most well known weapon of it's type in the world.
data.name: Common

data.flavor: The standard NATO assault weapon for battlefield work. Bullpup design, collapsing stock.
data.name: Common

data.flavor: A high powered auto-shotgun with lethal firepower. Used by Arasaka worldwide. Another good reason to avoid the Boys in Black.
data.name: Common

data.flavor: Light duty stakeout shotgun, used by city police departments.
data.name: Common

data.flavor: Close in Assault Weapon, designed for house to house work, crowd suppression. Scope is built in, making it very accurate for type.
data.name: Common

data.flavor: Uzi's entry into the 21st century, all plastic, with a rotary electric clip and adjustable trigger. The choice for many security Solos.
data.name: Common

data.flavor: Sternmeyer's best entry in the antiterrorist category, with wide use on CSWAT teams and PsychoSquads.
data.name: Common

data.flavor: Heckler & Koch's updating of the MPSK classic, with compound plastics and built in silencing.
data.name: Common

data.flavor: Standard US military SMG during WWII, the Thompson is rugged, reliable and easy to use. The M1928 version, of gangster fame, was less reliable (UR), but could carry a 50 round drum magazine.
data.name: Common

data.flavor: Updated MAC-10, with composite body and cylindrical feeding magazine.
data.name: Common

data.flavor: A bull pup configured SMG designed to be fired one handed. The Bushmaster uses the M-1 6A 1 clip, making it technically closer to an assault rifle than a submachinegun.
data.name: Common

data.flavor: A very small SMG used by covert units and terrorists. It can be easily silenced. However, its very large ammo size makes it very difficult to control when on full auto.
data.name: Common

data.flavor: The standard Arasaka Security weapon, found worldwide. A good, all round weapon.
data.name: Common

data.flavor: Possibly the most used Solo's gun in existence, the MPK-11 can be modified into four different designs, including a bullpup configuration, standard SMG, an assault carbine, and a grenade launcher mount.
data.name: Common

data.flavor: Two examples of the H&K family of interchangeable SMGs, both share parts and design similarities. The MP5K is a very small version of the MP5SD3, which has a built in silencer.
data.name: Common

data.flavor: A standard military sidearm for the Soviet Bloc, the Skorpion is the world's smallest military SMG. It's small ammunition size gives it excellent controllability. It is easily silenced and can be carried in a shoulder holster.
data.name: Common

data.flavor: Two examples of the H&K family of interchangeable SMGs, both share parts and design similarities. The MP5K is a very small version of the MP5SD3, which has a built in silencer.
data.name: Common

data.flavor: Developed by the Israelis as a reliable export weapon, the UZI is used worldwide by security forces, the US Secret Service, police and (unfortunately) terrorists and drug dealers.
data.name: Common

data.flavor: Standard US military SMG during WWII, the Thompson is rugged, reliable and easy to use. The M1928 version, of gangster fame, was less reliable (UR), but could carry a 50 round drum magazine.
data.name: Common

data.flavor: An updated version of the venerable Tech Assault I, features larger clip, better autofire, no melting. Honest.
data.name: Common

data.flavor: A light composite submachinegun with integral sights. Used by many Euro Solos.
data.name: Common

data.flavor: Grey block of plastique, can be detonated by timer, tripwire or signal.
data.name: Common

data.flavor: The third generation of the Stinger missile launcher, this shoulder arm fires one missile.
data.name: Common

data.flavor: Shoulder-mounted, rocket-powered grenade launcher. Heavily used in the Central American conflicts under the name RPG-A.
data.name: Common

data.flavor: Can be detonated by timer, tripwire, signal or motion detector.
data.name: Common

data.flavor: The cyberpsycho hunter's favorite. Almost 2 meters long, this "cannon" fires a depleted uranium shell at supersonic speeds. Heavy AP subcaliber penetrator damages armor 2pts/hit.
data.name: Common

data.flavor: Liquified napalm sprayer. Back mounted and bulky. Does extra damage following initial hit (see FNFF, pg.110).
data.name: Common

data.flavor: Launcher may be attached (under barrel) to any assault rifle, or handheld. Not compatible with Militech RPG-A.
data.name: Common

data.flavor: This is a large gauntlet covering the hand and forearm. It does 3D6 in crush damage, 2D6 punch damage, and has three spaces which can be used to store any standard cyberarm option.
data.name: Common

data.flavor: Mono-sectional crystal blade. Incredibly sharp. In the Japanese "naginata" style.
data.name: Common

data.flavor: Sword length version of monoblade. Resembles a hightech katana with a milky, nearly transparent blade.
data.name: Common

data.flavor: Mono-sectional crystal blade. Incredibly sharp. In the Japanese "tanto" style.
data.name: Common

system.flavor: gun hidden in arm/leg base on Body Type. Cost range 200-800eb
